findings.evidence: grep hits: line 755 says `The fundamental resolvent identity`; the theorem statement at lines 761-769 proves only `lambda • S.resolvent ... x - S.generator ⟨S.resolvent ... x, ...⟩ = x`; line 758 explicitly says the left inverse/injectivity is not proved.
findings.fix: Rename the docstring wording to “right-inverse relation” or “resolvent right inverse”, and adjust the module summary line 22 if keeping “resolvent identities” plural.
findings.issue: The docstring for `StronglyContinuousSemigroup.resolventRightInv` calls `(λI - A) R(λ) x = x` the “fundamental resolvent identity”, which is misleading: the theorem only states the right-inverse relation, not the usual resolvent identity/equation.

summary: The added documentation is mostly present, but it overclaims the resolvent material by calling a one-sided right-inverse theorem a resolvent identity.
